name: Jump Scare Trigger
description: A scripted or reactive scare event that uses timing, framing, audio, animation, and player state to create a sudden startle.
design_notes: Jump scares are strongest when they support pacing rather than replace tension. The trigger should define who can fire it, what state suppresses it, how often it can repeat, which camera and audio cues play, and how reduced-intensity settings alter the result. False positives and repeated scares quickly damage trust.
design_purpose: Create a sharp tension spike at a designed reveal, mistake, or scripted beat.
Teach players that certain spaces, sounds, or behaviors are dangerous.
Support pacing directors by punctuating long quiet stretches.
player_fantasy: The player feels shocked by a sudden reveal while still believing the scare belonged to the space and situation.

parameters.name: jump_scare_trigger_id
parameters.description: Stable scare trigger identifier used by persistence and analytics.

parameters.name: trigger_volume_id
parameters.description: Spatial or interaction trigger that arms the scare.

parameters.name: scare_rearm_delay_ms
parameters.description: Lockout window before the scare can fire again, if repeatable.

parameters.name: scripted_event_id
parameters.description: Authored event played after trigger validation.

parameters.name: one_shot_state_id
parameters.description: Persistence state for one-shot or conditional scares.

parameters.name: camera_cue_id
parameters.description: Camera presentation cue chosen for the scare.

edge_cases: The player enters the trigger while already in a cutscene, menu, hiding animation, or death state.
Reduced scare mode disables audio spikes but the camera cue still snaps violently.
A scare trigger fires repeatedly because one-shot state was not persisted after reload.
The player looks away from the intended reveal and sees only the camera movement or audio cue.
Multiple scare triggers overlap in a hallway and play competing stingers.
common_bugs: The scare plays after the threat has been removed or the scripted actor is pooled elsewhere.
Trigger validation uses camera position instead of player state and fires through walls.
Audio and animation are not synchronized, making the scare feel broken instead of intentional.
One-shot state is saved after the scare animation, so quitting early allows repeated farming.
Accessibility intensity changes apply to UI but not to existing loaded scare events.
balancing_notes: Repeated jump scares lose impact quickly; pacing should leave space for anticipation and recovery.
Low-threat false alarms can teach location language, but too many make real threats feel arbitrary.
Scares should rarely punish careful play without prior telegraphing.
accessibility_notes: Provide reduced jump scare mode that lowers audio spikes, camera snaps, flash intensity, or sudden motion.
Avoid mandatory rapid input immediately after a scare unless alternate settings exist.
Respect photosensitivity settings for flashes and high-contrast cuts.

implementation_notes.unity: Use trigger_volume_id plus explicit player state checks before firing scripted_event_id. Persist one_shot_state_id before starting animation, route camera_cue_id and audio through accessibility intensity settings, and guard against pooled actor references.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D nodes to arm triggers and a central scare director to check state, rearm delay, and intensity tier. Save fired state immediately so reloads do not repeat one-shot scares.
implementation_notes.unreal: Blueprint triggers can call a scare component, but validation should check player state, difficulty, intensity settings, and one-shot persistence first. Sequencer, camera shake, and audio cues should branch based on reduced-scare policy.
implementation_notes.web: In Phaser or custom JavaScript, store trigger state in level save data and schedule audio, camera, and sprite events from one scare record. Avoid relying on CSS or rendering callbacks to mark the scare as fired.

example_games.title: Five Nights at Freddy's
example_games.note: Uses sudden scare events as a major failure and tension beat.

example_games.title: Resident Evil 7: Biohazard
example_games.note: Uses scripted scares and sudden reveals in authored horror sequences.

example_games.title: Alien: Isolation
example_games.note: Uses sudden monster appearances and failure moments as authored scare beats.
